programmes.show_description: Who better to present a definitive countdown of 80s synthpop classics than OMD frontman Andy McCluskey? From Kraftwerk, Ultravox & Gary Numan to New Order, Yazoo and Pet Shop Boys.
Before the CD, the perfect pop songs were spun by singers and bands on 7 inch vinyl. From 1980-1984 these were the classic UK hits that invaded the world!
With limited chart success in the UK, we sometimes forget how huge Hall & Oates were in the early '80s - worldwide, they became the most successful duo of all time!

programmes.show_name: Make Mine A Double with Pat Sharp!
programmes.show_description: They say 2 heads are better than one & in the 80s the charts were full of collaborations. So pour a large one & have double the fun with Pat Sharp's top 40 duets.
All '80s - 24/7 - the feelgood pop decade where the Brits invaded the world!

programmes.show_name: 20 Big Selling US Songs of the 80s
programmes.show_description: Which singles by US acts sold more in the UK than any other in the '80s? We reveal all over two hours - you'll be surprised who's missing, and by the number 1!

120 videos, in order, a forgotten hit from each and every month of the '80s. Radio 1 legend Bruno Brookes is the man with the 10-hour musical plan!

programmes.show_description: Another episode of the Heritage Chart - new music by heritage acts! With Mike Read.

Sixty minutes of acts that had a single smash hit number 1, then never darkened the top 20 ever again! From Joe Dolce and Kelly Marie in 1980 to Robin Beck at the end of the decade.

programmes.show_name: Rustie Lee's Eat To The Beat!
programmes.show_description: Food glorious food! The 70s and 80s were full of edible bands and songs, and TV chef Rustie Lee has raided the fridge and pantry to serve up pop's sweet treats. It's going to be a riot!
